text: Overbooked
A layover
Ground staff
A connecting flight
To be rebooked
To be bumped
Standby
To misplace
To clear security
The departure lounge
Baggage claim
Customs
Jet lag
I was bumped from the flight.
She said the flight was delayed.
They said they would rebook me.
He said the plane had landed.
The agent told me she could help me.
Excuse me, I think there is a problem. My flight was delayed, and I have just missed my connecting flight to Porto.
I am sorry to hear that. Let me check. Yes, I can see the incoming flight arrived late. Do not worry, we will rebook you free of charge.
Thank you, that is a relief. Will the next flight have a seat, or will I be on standby?
You have a confirmed seat. There is a two-hour layover, but I will give you a voucher for the departure lounge.
That is very kind. One more thing, I am worried the airline might misplace my suitcase during the change.
I have tagged it through to Porto, so it will travel with you. Just collect it at baggage claim when you land.

Good afternoon, passengers. This is an important announcement for travellers on the four-fifteen service to Lisbon. We are sorry to inform you that your departure has been delayed by two hours due to the late arrival of the incoming aircraft. Passengers with a connecting flight should speak to the ground staff at the desk near gate twelve, who will help you rebook your onward journey at no extra charge. The departure lounge has been moved from the main hall to the upper level, just past security. Please keep your boarding pass and travel documents ready, and listen for further announcements. We thank you for your patience and we apologise for any inconvenience.

Honestly, what should have been a simple trip turned into a small adventure. At the desk, the agent told me the flight was overbooked, and she asked whether I would take a later one in exchange for a voucher. I said I would, since I had a long layover anyway. Then she warned me that my bag might be on standby and that I ought to check baggage claim carefully. In the end, the airline misplaced nothing, the connecting flight was smooth, and I even fast-tracked through customs. So now, whenever a trip goes wrong at first, I just tell myself it will probably make a better story later.
